seo_description: Current time in Accra, Ghana — live Greenwich Mean Time (GMT, UTC+0) clock, no DST, and time differences with Lagos, London, and world cities.

faq.question: What time is it in Accra right now?
faq.answer: Accra, Ghana uses Greenwich Mean Time (GMT, UTC+0) year-round. Ghana does not observe Daylight Saving Time. Accra is one of the few capital cities in the world that stays on UTC+0 permanently. The IANA timezone identifier is Africa/Accra.

faq.question: What time zone is Accra in?
faq.answer: Accra is in the GMT timezone (UTC+0) year-round. Ghana is geographically close to the prime meridian (the Greenwich Meridian passes through eastern Ghana). The IANA identifier is Africa/Accra. Accra shares its timezone with Abidjan, Dakar, Monrovia, and Reykjavik — all permanently on UTC+0.

faq.question: Does Accra observe daylight saving time?
faq.answer: No. Ghana does not observe Daylight Saving Time. Accra remains on GMT (UTC+0) year-round. Ghana has never observed DST.

faq.question: What is the time difference between Accra and London?
faq.answer: Accra (GMT, UTC+0) and London (GMT, UTC+0) are on the same time in winter. During UK summer (BST, UTC+1), London is 1 hour ahead of Accra. Since Ghana has no DST, the gap shifts between 0 hours (winter) and 1 hour (summer when the UK observes BST).

faq.question: What is the time difference between Accra and Lagos?
faq.answer: Accra (GMT, UTC+0) is 1 hour behind Lagos (WAT, UTC+1). Nigeria does not observe DST, and neither does Ghana, so this 1-hour gap is constant year-round.

faq.question: What is the time difference between Accra and New York?
faq.answer: Accra (GMT, UTC+0) is 5 hours ahead of New York (EST, UTC-5) in winter. During US Daylight Saving Time (EDT, UTC-4), Accra is 4 hours ahead. Since Ghana has no DST, the gap changes only when the US transitions.

faq.question: What are typical business hours in Accra?
faq.answer: Standard business hours in Accra are 8:00 AM to 5:00 PM GMT, Monday through Friday. Accra is the capital and largest city of Ghana. Ghana was the first sub-Saharan African country to gain independence (1957) and has one of the continent's most stable democracies. Major industries include gold mining, cocoa, oil, and a growing tech and fintech sector. The Ghana Stock Exchange (GSE) operates in Accra. Accra is also becoming a hub for African-American diaspora tourism (the 'Year of Return').
